But the problem is that inserting a new element into a heap. Let me think. Inserting into a heap can be done by pushing the element into the end. import heapq

def heap_replace(heap, value_to_remove, value_to_insert):
    # Remove the smallest element
    if len(heap) <= 1:
        return []
    # Create a copy of the heap
    new_heap = [element for element in heap]
    # Remove the smallest element
    heapq.heappop(new_heap)
    # Insert the new element
    heapq.heappush(new_heap, value_to_insert)
    return new_heap

arguments.code:
import heapq

def heap_replace(heap, value_to_remove, value_to_insert):
    # Remove the smallest element
    new_heap = [element for element in heap]
    heapq.heappop(new_heap)
    heapq.heappush(new_heap, value_to_insert)
    return new_heap

# Test cases
assert heap_replace([25, 44, 68, 21, 39, 23, 89], 21) == [21, 25, 23, 44, 39, 68, 89]
assert heap_replace([25, 44, 68, 21, 39, 23, 89], 110) == [23, 25, 68, 44, 39, 110, 89]
assert heap_replace([25, 44, 68, 21, 39, 23, 89], 500) == [23, 25, 68, 44, 39, 500, 89]

import heapq

def heap_replace(heap, value_to_remove, value_to_insert):
    # Remove the smallest element
    new_heap = [element for element in heap]
    heapq.heappop(new_heap)
    heapq.heappush(new_heap, value_to_insert)
    return new_heap

# Test cases
assert heap_replace([25, 44, 68, 21, 39, 23, 89], 21) == [21, 25, 23, 44, 39, 68, 89]
assert heap_replace([25, 44, 68, 21, 39, 23, 89], 110) == [23, 25, 68, 44, 39, 110, 89]
assert heap_replace([25, 44, 68, 21, 39, 23, 89], 500) == [23, 25, 68, 44, 39, 500, 89]
